arts.ttl: Noel Hunt on the prospect of 'fantastic' returning former Reading star with Stockport
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has said 'the time for chat is afterwards' ahead of a reunion with former Royal, Ollie Norwood. The 33-year-old has been an EFL and Premier League fixture for over 15 years, most notably with Sheffield United, and made nearly a century of appearances for Reading between 2014 and 2016. Joining Stockport County in the summer upon their promotion from League Two, the Northern Ireland legend has been a regular as the Hatters sit seventh, directly behind sixth-placed Reading. Set to return to the Select Car Leasing Stadium on Saturday, Hunt said it will be nice to see the former Manchester United youngster, but will save niceties until post-match. "He is a fantastic player, everyone in the EFL and above will know how good a player Ollie Norwood is. It’ll be great to see him come back to the club, but we’ve got to worry about ourselves.

arts.ttl: Noel Hunt issues update as illness sweeps through Reading camp ahead of Stockport
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has confirmed that some members of his squad are struggling with illness as attention turns to Saturday's clash with Stockport County. Mirroring many workplaces outside of football, seasonal flu and an increase in bugs have began to impact all walks of life, and football is no different. Attempting to contain it as best they can, players struggling have been kept away from the rest of the camp but bugs are still spreading, rendering some players doubtful for Saturday's clash with play-off rivals Stockport. "It’s been going through the camp a little bit, but we’ve tried to isolate it best we can so the ones that have had it are away from the club and back in," Hunt told the media in the build-up to the match.
